title: Neimoidian Royal Guard
content:
{{Top|legends=Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion}} {{Otheruses|Royal Guard|more=gunner}} {{Update|[[Brotherhood (novel)|Brotherhood]]}} {{Organization |image=[[File:NeimoidianRoyalGuard-SWBC58.png]] |name=Neimoidian Royal Guard |type=[[Infantry]]''[[Rise of the Separatists]]'' |founder= |leader= |subgroup= |headquarters= |locations= |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=*[[Cato Neimoidian government]][[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] *[[Trade Federation]]{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}} **[[Trade Federation Home Defense Legions]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]{{Film|III}} **[[Confederacy military]] *[[House Maccon]][[Poe Dameron 20|''Poe Dameron'' 20]] }} {{Quote|Guards aren't supposed to engage with passersby during our duty. It's disruptive.''"
"''I appreciate the hard work of loyal guards. My apologies for getting in the way of your tasks. If, however, anyone wishes to discuss the previous transgressions of the Republic… I offer a sympathetic ear. And I will be easy to find.|Guardswoman Ruug Quarnom and Separatist Asajj Ventress, who aimed her response at guardsman Ketar Nor|}} The '''Neimoidian Royal Guard''', whose members were known as '''Neimoidian guards''', '''Neimoidian soldiers''', or '''Neimoidian gunners''', was a military branch of the [[Cato Neimoidian government|government]] of the [[Neimoidian]] [[Purse world|Purse World]] [[Cato Neimoidia]]. Members of the Guard also served the [[Trade Federation]] and [[Confederacy military]]. ==Description== Organized under the [[Trade Federation Home Defense Legions]], the Neimoidian Royal Guard was a [[Neimoidian]] security force composed of personnel identified as Neimoidian guards,{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/sphere-of-influence-concept-art-gallery|text="Sphere of Influence" Concept Art Gallery}} {{C|Slide 6}} Neimoidian [[soldier]]s, or Neimoidian [[gunner]]s.''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' The Royal Guard served as a security branch within both the [[Cato Neimoidian government]] and [[Trade Federation]] corporation. As a result of the Trade Federation's alliance with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], something that was officially covered up by the Federation's supposed neutrality, members of the Neimoidian Royal Guard found themselves serving the [[Confederacy military]] while others, such as those within the Cato Neimoidian government or serving more neutral Federation personnel, found themselves outside the CIS military. The Neimoidian [[House Maccon]] also maintained a force of guards. ==History== {{Quote|The detention block is closed to visitors. Move along.|A Neimoidian guard, to Ahsoka Tano and Riyo Chuchi|}} After questioning the [[Trade Federation]], [[Ruug Quarnom]] was discharged from the [[Neimoidian special forces]] and assigned to the Royal Guard on [[Cato Neimoidia]], where she was assigned the young guard [[Ketar Nor]] as her partner, with Quarnom becoming a mentor to her partner. When a [[Cadesura disaster|massive bombing]], secretly orchestrated by the [[Sith]], rocked the [[Cadesura district]], the [[Galactic Republic]] dispatched [[Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to clear its name and [[Mission to Cato Neimoidia (Kenobi and Skywalker)|investigate]], while the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] sent [[Commander]] [[Asajj Ventress]]. Ventress managed to convince Nor, who already hated the Republic for its anti-Neimoidian prejudices, to begin a new bombing campaign designed to push his people to the Separatists, but he was stopped by the Jedi and Quarnom. While the [[Cato Neimoidian government]] remained neutral, Quarnom was branded a traitor thanks to Nor's testimony, but she was [[Rescue of Ruug Quarnom|rescued]] from [[battle droid]]s by Kenobi and [[Anakin Skywalker]]. [[File:Neimoidian_Guard.png|thumb|left|230px|A Neimoidian soldier during the Blockade of Pantora]] In [[21 BBY]],{{TCWcanondates|304}} Neimoidian guards were stationed onboard the Trade Federation's [[command ship]] during the [[Blockade of Pantora]]. Some of them were given their own offices aboard the [[Starship|vessel]]. Jedi Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano]] was able to [[mind trick]] a [[Unidentified Neimoidian guard|guard]] into letting her and senator [[Riyo Chuchi]] enter the detention block. Following the freeing of the kidnapped [[Chi Eekway Papanoida|Chi Eekway]], the Federation's [[Unidentified Neimoidian envoy|envoy]] and a squad of Neimoidian soldiers arrived at the scene to stop them. To avoid a scandal with the Republic over the [[Sib Canay]]'s scheme, the envoy allowed them to leave and convinced the higher-ups to end the blockade. Neimoidian soldiers were also part of the [[Trade Federation Home Defense Legions]] that made up the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]' forces that fought against the Republic's [[2nd Fleet]], which had blockaded the [[Quellor sector]] where the Federation's [[purse world]]s were. Several of Neimoidian guards were present at a [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility|mining complex]] on [[Mustafar]] protecting the [[Executive Separatist Council|Separatist Council]]. However, when [[Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] [[Mission to Mustafar|arrived]] under orders from his [[Sith Master]], [[Darth Sidious]], to [[Death|dispose]] of the Separatist Council, the [[Sith apprentice]] slew all present. During the [[New Republic Era]], [[House Maccon]] employed a number of Neimoidian Royal Guards as security personnel. In [[34 ABY]], [[Lor San Tekka]] traveled to [[Cato Neimoidia]] and entered the [[Great Vaults of Cato Neimoidia|Palace of Baron Paw Maccon]], disguised as a visitor on a guided tour. However, after finding the [[Kazerath]] device which he sought he was arrested by Neimoidian guards. ==Equipment== The general attire of the Neimoidian Royal Guard was a suit of bronze armor completed with a [[helmet]] of the same [[color]]. The gear was a heavy suit of armor, but it was lighter than the armor worn by [[Neimoidian heavy troop]]s. The standard weaponry of the guard was a type of long [[rifle]]. Nonetheless, the Neimoidian Guards aboard the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship|Droid Control Ship]] orbiting the moon of Pantora wore grey sharp-edged pauldrons, gauntlets, and helmets, as well as bluish light armor with symbols of the Trade Federation on them. For weaponry, they wielded [[Weequay blaster pistol]]s. [[Commando]]s in the [[Neimoidian special forces]] wore maroon armor. The formfitting commando gear blended the mobility of standard guard armor with the protection of heavy [[trooper]]s thanks to its plating. During the [[Cold War]] between the [[First Order]] and the [[Resistance]], Neimoidian guards in the employ of House Maccon wore grey heavy armor with bronze helmets and the long rifles. ==Behind the scenes== During the development of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], one predecessor to the Neimoidian Guard design used the [[Abyssin]] species previously seen in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. This design, called simply the "Separatist Alien Guard," was depicted in concept art and later included on the ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' blu-ray collection.''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'', Disc 7, Star Wars Archives: Episode III Separatist Alien Guard Concept Like other unique alien soldiers seen in the film, the Neimoidian guard needed specifically designed guns for their organization, so that work was done by [[Ty Teiger]]'s team. Teiger later recalled making Neimoidian guns as an example of how work on ''Revenge of the Sith'' was "more specific" than work on the previous [[prequel trilogy]] films, which had comparatively more background characters to focus on. Using the Neimoidian gun as an example, Teiger broke down the creative process in an interview with ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: Official Episode III Souvenir Movie Guide]]'', explaining that he would receive a base sketch, which he then passed out to twenty propmakers, each of whom had their own creative approach to making the weapon, allowing Director [[George Lucas]] to pick from a diverse spread of options.{{StoryCite|book=Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: Official Episode III Souvenir Movie Guide|story=Ty Teiger: Weapons of War}} ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars Journeys: Beginnings]]'' *[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] {{Ab|Brotherhood (audiobook)}} {{1stID|Neimoidian Royal Guard}} *{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}} *{{Film|III}} {{1st}} *[[Darth Vader (2020) 7|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 7]] {{Corpse}} *[[Darth Vader (2020) 24|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 24]] {{Flash}} *[[Darth Vader (2020) 33|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 33]] {{Corpse}} {{Flash}} *[[Poe Dameron 20|''Poe Dameron'' 20]] ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Three|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' The Complete Season Three]] *{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/sphere-of-influence-episode-guide|text="Sphere of Influence" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}} {{1stID|Neimoidian guard}} *''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' {{1stID|Neimoidian gunner}} *''[[Rise of the Separatists]]'' {{1stID| Neimoidian soldier}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Guardia neimoidiano |fr=Soldat Neimoidien }} [[Category:Organizations of Cato Neimoidia]] [[Category:Separatist military units]] [[Category:Trade Federation military units]]
